specs.section_path: 15-Layer Summary Table > 4.3 The Origin of Purpose: Causal Entropic Forces & Negative Entropy > The Physical Equivalence of Sustainable Ethical Coherence and Causal Path Entropy > 4. The Formal Equivalence of SEC and SC​ > 4.1 The Composite Functional for Causal Path Entropy
source_formulae: P0R03818: 4.1 The Composite Functional for Causal Path Entropy

P0R03819: We can construct a functional form for SC that makes its dependence on the metrics for C, K, and Q explicit. The total "volume" of the accessible path space, Wpaths, can be approximated as the product of three factors: the number of distinguishable states the system can occupy (Nstates), the fraction of these states that are dynamically accessible and stable (facc), and a factor representing the diversity of the path types (Dpaths).

P0R03822: Nstates is a monotonically increasing function of Complexity (K), as measured by . | facc is maximised when Coherence (C) is tuned to the quasicritical point. | Dpaths is a monotonically increasing function of Qualia Capacity (Q), as measured by the topological complexity of the state space.
P0R03823: The causal path entropy is the logarithm of this volume:
P0R03824: $SC = kB ln(Wpaths) \approx kB\left( \ln\left( Nstates(K) \right) + ln\left( facc(C) \right) + ln\left( Dpaths(Q) \right) \right)$
P0R03825: This expression demonstrates that SC is a composite function that is maximised only when K, C, and Q are jointly maximised.
